heading: Wildfire resilience projects
label: 6591d Wildfire resilience projects

children.text: carried out in accordance with subsections (b), (c), and (d) of and sections 6514 and 6515 of this title;section 6512 of this title
considered an action categorically excluded from the requirements of ( et seq.); andPublic Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321
exempt from the special administrative review process under .section 6515 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: maximizes the retention of old-growth and large trees, as appropriate for the forest type, to the extent that the trees promote stands that are resilient to insects and disease, and reduce the risk or extent of, or increase the resilience to, wildfires;

children.label: B
children.text: considers the best available scientific information to maintain or restore the ecological integrity, including maintaining or restoring structure, function, composition, and connectivity; and

children.label: i
children.text: includes multiple interested persons representing diverse interests; and

children.label: I
children.text: is transparent and nonexclusive; or

children.label: II
children.text: meets the requirements for a resource advisory committee under subsections (c) through (f) of .section 7125 of this title

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: 2 Inclusion
children.text: A project under this subsection may carry out part of a proposal that complies with the eligibility requirements of the Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Program under .section 7303(b) of this title

children.heading: Project size
children.label: 1 Project size
children.text: A project under this section may not exceed 3000 acres.

children.label: A
children.text: Prioritized within the wildland-urban interface;

children.label: B
children.text: If located outside the wildland-urban interface, limited to areas within Condition Classes 2 or 3 in Fire Regime Groups I, II, or III that contain very high wildfire hazard potential; and

children.label: C
children.text: Limited to areas designated under as of .section 6591a(b) of this titleMarch 23, 2018

children.heading: Prohibition on establishment
children.label: i Prohibition on establishment
children.text: A project under this section shall not include the establishment of permanent roads.

children.heading: Existing roads
children.label: ii Existing roads
children.text: The Secretary may carry out necessary maintenance and repairs on existing permanent roads for the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Temporary roads
children.label: B Temporary roads
children.text: The Secretary shall decommission any temporary road constructed under a project under this section not later than 3 years after the date on which the project is completed.

children.heading: Extraordinary circumstances
children.label: 4 Extraordinary circumstances
children.text: The Secretary shall apply the extraordinary circumstances procedures under section 220.6 of title 36, code of Federal regulations (or successor regulations), when using the categorical exclusion under this section.1So in original. Probably should be “Code of Federal Regulations”.1
a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System;
any Federal land on which, by Act of Congress or Presidential proclamation, the removal of vegetation is restricted or prohibited;
a congressionally designated wilderness study area; or
an area in which activities under subsection (a) would be inconsistent with the applicable land and resource management plan.

children.heading: Forest management plans
children.label: e Forest management plans
children.text: All projects and activities carried out under this section shall be consistent with the land and resource management plan established under for the unit of the National Forest System containing the projects and activities.section 1604 of this title

children.heading: Public notice and scoping
children.label: f Public notice and scoping
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct public notice and scoping for any project or action proposed in accordance with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall prepare an annual report on the use of categorical exclusions under this section that includes a description of all acres (or other appropriate unit) treated through projects carried out under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives;

children.label: D
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: E
children.text: the Government Accountability Office.
